name: Heartland Bank And Trust Company
description_short: Community bank with 86 branches across Illinois, Iowa, and Missouri, offering checking, savings, wealth management, and agricultural services since 1920.
description_long:
Heartland Bank and Trust Company traces its roots to 1920 when M.B. Drake founded a small community bank in Cornland, Illinois. The Drake family, who arrived in Central Illinois in 1852, built the institution through multiple generations, surviving economic cycles and expanding into a regional banking presence. Today, the bank operates 86 branch locations across Illinois and eastern Iowa and Missouri, maintaining the family tradition of community-focused banking established over a century ago.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail and commercial banking services including checking and savings accounts, online banking, mobile banking with bill pay functionality, wealth management services, and specialized agricultural services. Customers can open accounts online and access banking services through digital channels, branch visits, or phone support. The bank emphasizes relationship-based banking, positioning itself as a solution provider that takes time to understand customer financial needs before recommending products.

Heartland Bank distinguishes itself through deep community roots, family ownership continuity, and regional focus rather than national scale. The bank ranked #1 on Forbes' 2026 America's Best Banks List, which management attributes to team dedication and commitment to serving customers with efficient, sustainable growth. The institution recently integrated CNB (Community National Bank) customers into its digital banking platform and branch network, suggesting recent acquisition or merger activity.

As a traditional community bank, Heartland Bank is best suited for customers prioritizing personalized service and local relationships over cutting-edge fintech features or the widest product variety. The bank's strength lies in understanding regional markets and building long-term customer relationships rather than competing on rates or innovative digital products. Prospective customers should verify specific product offerings, rates, and fees directly, as the website focuses on relationship philosophy rather than detailed product specifications.
services: Mobile banking app with bill pay
pros: Ranked #1 on Forbes' 2026 America's Best Banks List
86 branch locations across Illinois, Iowa, and Missouri for in-person banking access
Over 100 years of community banking history with family ownership since 1920
Mobile banking app available for iOS and Android with bill pay capabilities
Specialized agricultural services tailored to regional farming communities
Online account opening for checking accounts
Wealth management services available for customer financial planning
Recently expanded customer base through CNB integration with unified digital banking access
cons: Limited geographic footprint confined to Illinois, Iowa, and Missouri—not available nationally
Website lacks specific information on interest rates, fees, APYs, or loan terms
No mention of premium checking features, credit card offerings, or investment products
Minimal details on customer service hours, response times, or support availability
best_for: Regional customers in Illinois, Iowa, and Missouri seeking personalized community banking relationships
Agricultural and farm business owners needing specialized lending and financial services
Customers prioritizing face-to-face banking relationships over digital-only neobank convenience
Individuals and families wanting banking from an institution with deep local community roots
diagnosis: Heartland Bank is best for regional customers in Illinois, Iowa, and Missouri who value community banking relationships and personalized service over national reach or aggressive digital innovation. The main caveat is geographic limitation—the bank only serves three states—and limited public transparency regarding rates, fees, and specific product terms on their website.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
